bead.title: B22d-f: SessionIndexEntryFromResult takes discrete args, not RunOptions
bead.description:
Step f/h of B22d decomposition.

Change SessionIndexEntryFromResult's RunOptions parameter to take only the small subset of fields it actually uses (Harness, Model, Provider, Correlation, PromptSource, etc.) as discrete arguments or a small internal struct. Update session_index_test.go and the four service-run callers in the same commit.

In-scope:
- cli/internal/agent/session_index.go SessionIndexEntryFromResult signature
- cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go
- cli/internal/agent/service_run.go callers

Out-of-scope:
- Other session_index changes
bead.acceptance: field RunOptions removed from cli/internal/agent/session_index.go. cd cli && go build ./... passes. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes.

events.body:
no_changes
rationale: Bead ddx-9f4b1a8c (B22d-f) cannot be landed in isolation against base 4267be6c.

What is done
- The signature change itself is straightforward and verified locally:
  SessionIndexEntryFromResult(projectRoot string, harness, model, provider, effort string,
                              correlation map[string]string, result *Result,
                              startedAt, endedAt time.Time) SessionIndexEntry
  - cli/internal/agent/session_index.go: removed the RunOptions field/parameter
    and rewired the body to read from the discrete params (harness/model/provider/
    effort/correlation). After this change, RunOptions no longer appears in
    session_index.go (AC #1).
  - The single existing caller in cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:257
    (the bead's "four service-run callers" overcounts; only one call site exists)
    was updated to:
      SessionIndexEntryFromResult(workDir, opts.Harness, opts.Model,
                                  opts.Provider, opts.Effort, opts.Correlation,
                                  result, start, finishedAt)
  - cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go (TestSessionIndexPreservesWorker-
    IDCorrelation, line ~195) was updated to call the new signature with
    discrete args. The test passes.
  - `cd cli && go build ./...` passes (AC #2).
  - `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` runs clean for everything that
    isn't TestIntegration_CodexEcho. That test fails for an unrelated reason
    (real codex CLI returns "thread not found") on this machine — it is not
    a regression introduced by this change, and -short does not skip it.

What is blocking
- The lefthook pre-commit `runtime-lint` rule (lefthook.yml) runs the
  SD-024 runtimelint analyzer and filters its diagnostics down to the set
  of staged files. Because the bead's in-scope files
  (cli/internal/agent/service_run.go, cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go)
  contain many out-of-scope RunOptions usages — RunViaService,
  runFixtureHarnessViaRunner, RunWithConfigViaService, RunViaServiceWith,
  the `opts := RunOptions{...}` composite at service_run.go:82, and the
  TestRunViaServiceWithAppendsOneSessionIndexRow composite at
  session_index_test.go:274 — the lint blocks the commit even though none
  of those usages are touched by this bead.
- The bead's stated out-of-scope explicitly excludes "service_run.go public
  API deletions (step h)" (per the parent step-d bead description). Those
  call sites and signatures are what the lint is flagging.
- Running the analyzer over ./internal/agent/... reports many other
  RunOptions references across the package (compare_adapter.go,
  execute_bead.go, execute_bead_review.go, grade.go, runner.go,
  agent_runner.go, agent_runner_service.go, claude_stream.go, models.go,
  etc.) that depend on later B22d steps and the broader Stage-4 retirement.
- Per project policy, --no-verify / hook-skipping is forbidden; per the
  bead instructions, modifying out-of-scope files is forbidden. There is no
  in-scope way to make the lint pass on the staged-file set.

What a follow-up attempt would need
- Land step h (or the broader RunOptions retirement) FIRST so that
  service_run.go and session_index_test.go no longer contain pre-existing
  RunOptions usages, then this bead can run and the lint will pass on the
  staged-file set; OR
- Re-decompose the lint scope: change runtime-lint in lefthook.yml to
  filter diagnostics to only the staged hunk lines (e.g. via a diff-aware
  pass) rather than the whole staged file, so a partial migration step
  isn't blocked by file-level peers; OR
- Expand this bead's in-scope to include the full RunOptions removal from
  service_run.go and session_index_test.go (effectively folding step h into
  step f), and re-queue with a model that can carry the broader change.

The diff that was prepared and then reverted is reproducible from the bead
description in 5 minutes; the blocker is project-level lint policy versus
bead scope, not implementation difficulty.
